item.title: OpenAI chief operating officer takes on new role in shake-up
item.description: Brad Lightcap assigned fresh responsibilities focusing on special projects as group prepares for an initial public offering

item.title: Meta-backed data centre seeks $3bn for campus with novel financing
item.description: ‘Project Walleye’ lenders would be first to fund both construction and power

item.title: The great Wall Street relocation that never quite happens
item.description: While Apollo seeks a second headquarters in the American south, demand for office space rises in New York

item.title: It’s far too easy to get sucked down a conspiracy theory rabbit hole
item.description: We all tend to accept information that is consistent with our prior beliefs, and reject the opposite

item.title: US fighter jet shot down over Iran
item.description: Search under way for two crew members after US F-15E brought down

item.title: Management consultants are ruining UK universities
item.description: Relentless off-the-peg commercial rewiring has undermined British higher education

item.title: Investors are betting on AI chaos. History suggests otherwise
item.description: The experience of past tech revolutions suggests savvy incumbents might muddle through and even thrive

item.title: Reeves’ ‘mansion tax’ to hit about 160,000 homes, says fiscal watchdog
item.description: OBR analysis estimates one-fifth of affected property owners will appeal revaluations

item.title: Turbulent markets fuel record year for Alex Gerko’s XTX
item.description: Billionaire’s trading business is one of the most profitable UK private companies

item.title: French-owned container ship braves passage through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: CMA CGM Kribi is the first western European vessel known to have transited since start of US-Israeli war on Iran

item.title: Trust fund battle shows the limits of the UK’s retail investing culture
item.description: If policymakers really want an active retail investing culture, they should remove the frictions

item.title: What’s going on with dollar debt?
item.description: ‘Swap’ trades by hedge funds have exploded recently

item.title: The hedge fund billionaire battle royale
item.description: The Two Sigma co-chief executive appointed to quell the feud between its founders quietly quit last month

item.title: UK motorists face £2 a litre diesel as Iran war drives wholesale prices to 4-year high
item.description: Scarce cargoes have boosted European diesel futures to equivalent of $211 a barrel, almost double the price of crude oil

item.title: Europe must prepare for ‘long-lasting’ energy shock, EU warns
item.description: Energy commissioner says bloc is assessing ‘all possibilities’ including fuel rationing and releasing more oil from strategic reserves

item.title: Leading UK climate scientists warn against new North Sea drilling
item.description: Intervention comes as political pressure rises for British government response to energy crisis caused by Iran war

item.title: Easter eggs stay pricey even as cocoa prices plunge
item.description: Manufacturers try to recoup some losses after margins were squeezed by spike in cocoa prices

item.title: ‘Epic’ cyclone Narelle spreads damage and disrupts mines and gas
item.description: Skies turned blood red in Western Australia ahead of tropical system that travelled for more than 6,500km

item.title: How Nelson Peltz chalked up another corporate break-up at Unilever
item.description: ‘Unbelievably pushy’ activist investor backed $66bn carve-out of century-old food business

item.title: Insurers turn to catastrophe bonds to offload data centre risks
item.description: Industry explores raising capital from alternative investors to cover AI mega-projects

item.title: Prediction markets: the hunt for the new ‘dumb money’
item.description: As new bettors flock to platforms such as Kalshi and Polymarket, some make easy prey for trading firms and professional gamblers

item.title: Kenya enters rare earths race with Mrima Hill deposit
item.description: Authorities seek intense competition from the US, China and Australia for ‘poster child’ project

item.title: How US anti-vaxxers found they were not immune to pushback
item.description: Recent setbacks for the movement signal a potential turning point for the vaccine makers as the market picks up

item.title: Robert Armstrong on coining the Taco trade
item.description: Markets went haywire after the ‘liberation day’ tariffs. Until investors started noticing a pattern

item.title: Oil cargo prices surge as fears of supply shortage grip market
item.description: Address by Trump signalling possible escalation of Iran conflict raises prospect of prolonged closure of Strait of Hormuz

item.title: M&S demands urgent action by ministers to tackle ‘brazen’ UK shoplifting
item.description: FTSE 100 retailer urges the government and London mayor to better support the police

item.title: FirstFT: Trump ousts US attorney-general Pam Bondi
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China hosts Pakistan-Afghanistan peace talks, and Blue Owl struck by $5.4bn of redemption requests

item.title: Willie Walsh is that rarity in India — a foreign chief executive
item.description: While Indians run some of the world’s biggest finance and tech companies, the country’s own aviation sector needs to import talent

item.title: How an energy crisis unfolds
item.description: The lasting effects of a best-case scenario

item.title: OpenAI acquires popular tech talk show for ‘low hundreds of millions’
item.description: ChatGPT-maker moves into broadcasting with deal for TBPN after it had pledged to abandon ‘side-quests’
